claims.summary: Cautilus keeps agent and workflow behavior honest while prompts keep changing.
claims.verbatim: `Cautilus` keeps agent and workflow behavior honest while prompts keep changing.

claims.summary: Cautilus is a repo-local contract layer that lets you define the behavior to protect once and verify it survives prompt, skill, and wrapper changes.
claims.verbatim: It is a repo-local contract layer for agent and workflow behavior evaluation: define the behavior you are trying to protect once, then verify it survives prompt, skill, and wrapper changes.
claims.edgeRationale: The protect-once-then-verify-survival promise is the APEX behavior contract, but it is realized through the evaluate surface that runs the bounded behavior fixture.

claims.summary: Cautilus ships as a standalone binary plus Cautilus Agent that a host repo can install without copying another scaffold first.
claims.verbatim: Ships as a standalone binary plus Cautilus Agent, which a host repo can install without copying another scaffold first.

claims.summary: Agents are first-class users of the product surface.
claims.verbatim: Agents are first-class users of the product surface.

claims.summary: Commands should emit durable packets with enough state for the next agent to resume, not only terminal prose for a human operator.
claims.verbatim: Commands should emit durable packets with enough state for the next agent to resume, not only terminal prose for a human operator.
claims.edgeRationale: Durable resumable packets are the orchestration design principle, but the same packet-output guarantee is what makes discovery and evaluate outputs reopenable.

claims.summary: Cautilus installs as a machine-level binary, but its agent-facing surface is intentionally repo-local.
claims.verbatim: `Cautilus` installs as a machine-level binary, but its agent-facing surface is intentionally repo-local.

claims.summary: The binary is shared across repos.
claims.verbatim: The binary is shared across repos.

claims.summary: The Cautilus Agent surface, adapter wiring, prompts, and instruction-routing surface are not shared across repos.
claims.verbatim: The Cautilus Agent surface, adapter wiring, prompts, and instruction-routing surface are not.

claims.summary: Agent surface, adapter wiring, prompts, and routing stay checked into each host repo so evaluation behavior remains reproducible, reviewable, and owned by the declaring repo.
claims.verbatim: They stay checked into each host repo so evaluation behavior remains reproducible, reviewable, and owned by the repo that declares it.
claims.edgeRationale: Repo-local checked-in surfaces are an install/ownership property, and they also underwrite the reproducible-and-reviewable evidence stance that proven-on-itself depends on.

claims.summary: The current external-adoption slice is eval-only while the broader claim, improve, live app-runner, and review-learning contracts are still being rewritten.
claims.verbatim: The current external-adoption slice is eval-only while the broader claim, improve, live app-runner, and review-learning contracts are still being rewritten.

claims.summary: Host repos can use cautilus evaluate fixture, evaluate observation, and post-run evaluate skill-experiment with checked-in fixtures, host-owned adapters, preserved task packets, and the current evaluation and skill-experiment report packets.
claims.verbatim: Host repos can use `cautilus evaluate fixture`, `cautilus evaluate observation`, and post-run `cautilus evaluate skill-experiment` with checked-in fixtures, host-owned adapters, preserved task packets, and the current evaluation and skill-experiment report packets.
claims.edgeRationale: fixture and observation runs are the evaluate surface, while skill-experiment compare is the scenarios/experiments surface, so the claim spans both eval epics.

claims.summary: skill-experiment compare compares host-preserved baseline and variant outputs; it does not clone, install, or execute skills.
claims.verbatim: `skill-experiment compare` compares host-preserved baseline and variant outputs; it does not clone, install, or execute skills.

claims.summary: Claim discovery automation, improve automation, live eval app-runner workflows, and review-learning packet capture or selected-packet summaries are opt-in product slices until the rewrite closes.
claims.verbatim: Treat claim discovery automation, improve automation, live `eval` app-runner workflows, and review-learning packet capture or selected-packet summaries as opt-in product slices until the rewrite closes.
claims.edgeRationale: The opt-in boundary covers claim discovery automation (discovery epic) and improve automation (bounded-improvement epic) together, so it scopes both.

claims.summary: Cautilus is not for repos that only need deterministic lint, unit, or type checks and do not have an evaluator-dependent behavior surface.
claims.verbatim: Not for: repos that only need deterministic lint, unit, or type checks and do not have an evaluator-dependent behavior surface.

claims.summary: If you hand setup to an agent, repeating cautilus doctor --repo-root . --next-action and doing what the packet says drives onboarding until cautilus doctor reports readiness plus a first_bounded_run.
claims.verbatim: If you want to hand setup to an agent, ask it to repeat `cautilus doctor --repo-root . --next-action`, do exactly what the packet says, and stop only after `cautilus doctor --repo-root .` reports readiness plus a `first_bounded_run`.
claims.edgeRationale: doctor --next-action reporting readiness and first_bounded_run is the readiness epic, while an agent repeating doctor and following the packet to drive onboarding is agent orchestration.

claims.summary: docs/specs/index.spec.md is the top-level proven-on-itself apex and the specdown entry; the user and maintainer spec indexes it links to remain the curated claim source of truth.
claims.verbatim: [docs/specs/index.spec.md](./docs/specs/index.spec.md) is the top-level "proven on itself" apex and the specdown entry; the user and maintainer spec indexes it links to remain the curated claim source of truth.

claims.summary: Raw discover claims packets remain the high-recall, source-ref-backed proof-planning input, not the primary document a user should review.
claims.verbatim: Raw `discover claims` packets remain the high-recall, source-ref-backed proof-planning input, not the primary document a user should review.

claims.summary: The Cautilus Agent curates the discovery packet against the repo: reducing false positives, raising likely missing public promises, and separating in-scope discovery bugs from out-of-scope narrative gaps.
claims.verbatim: The Cautilus Agent curates that packet against the repo: reduce false positives, raise likely missing public promises, and separate in-scope discovery bugs from out-of-scope narrative gaps.
claims.edgeRationale: Curating false positives and missing promises is the agent curation/review epic, while the artifact being curated is the discovery packet that the discovery epic produces.

claims.summary: The public website report is generated from the claim spec tree, but host repos do not need that renderer before Cautilus can inspect readiness, claims, evals, or improvement work.
claims.verbatim: The public website report is generated from the claim spec tree, but host repos do not need that renderer before Cautilus can inspect readiness, claims, evals, or improvement work.

claims.summary: Each claim page pairs a bounded product promise with executable evidence or an explicit evidence gap.
claims.verbatim: Each claim page pairs a bounded product promise with executable evidence or an explicit evidence gap.

claims.summary: This bounded eval loop verifies a bounded behavior fixture and produces reopenable observed and summary packets.
claims.verbatim: This loop verifies a bounded behavior fixture and produces reopenable observed and summary packets.

claims.summary: Cautilus turns the fixture run into durable eval packets that another agent or maintainer can reopen.
claims.verbatim: `Cautilus` turns the fixture run into durable eval packets that another agent or maintainer can reopen.

claims.summary: The eval summary is not a global product verdict; it is evidence for the behavior fixture and adapter path that the host repo chose.
claims.verbatim: The summary is not a global product verdict; it is evidence for the behavior fixture and adapter path that the host repo chose.

claims.summary: Cautilus turns behavior evidence into a reviewable decision surface.
claims.verbatim: It is the shortest currently stable external-adoption example of the product claim: `Cautilus` turns behavior evidence into a reviewable decision surface.
claims.edgeRationale: Turning behavior evidence into a reviewable decision surface is the apex product claim, realized through the evaluate surface that produces that evidence.

claims.summary: In charness, an instruction-surface fixture proved that the agent first selected the startup bootstrap helper find-skills, then selected the durable work skill for the actual task.
claims.verbatim: In `charness`, an instruction-surface fixture proved that the agent first selected the startup bootstrap helper `find-skills`, then selected the durable work skill for the actual task.
claims.edgeRationale: The dogfood result is proven-on-itself evidence, and it is produced by running an instruction-surface fixture through the evaluate surface.

claims.summary: The dogfood run turned the question of whether the agent read and followed the repo instructions from transcript judgment into a reproducible packet with artifacts another maintainer can reopen.
claims.verbatim: That turned "did the agent read and follow the repo instructions?" from transcript judgment into a reproducible packet with artifacts another maintainer can reopen.
claims.edgeRationale: Converting transcript judgment into a reproducible reopenable packet is proven-on-itself evidence delivered through the evaluate packet surface.

claims.summary: The dogfood run exposed a useful limit: routing proof is not backend subagent capability proof.
claims.verbatim: The same dogfood run also exposed a useful limit: routing proof is not backend subagent capability proof.

claims.summary: Keeping the routing-vs-capability distinction in the packet prevented the result from over-claiming what had been verified.
claims.verbatim: Keeping that distinction in the packet prevented the result from over-claiming what had been verified.

claims.summary: Claim discovery turns adapter-owned entry docs and linked Markdown into cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1 candidates.
claims.verbatim: Claim discovery turns adapter-owned entry docs and linked Markdown into `cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1` candidates.

claims.summary: Claim discovery is proof planning, not a verdict that the repo is correct.
claims.verbatim: It is proof planning, not a verdict that the repo is correct.

claims.summary: The Cautilus Agent curates false positives, likely missing promises, scan boundaries, and extraction and review budgets before any eval plan is trusted.
claims.verbatim: The Cautilus Agent curates false positives, likely missing promises, scan boundaries, and extraction and review budgets before any eval plan is trusted.
claims.edgeRationale: Agent curation of false positives and missing promises is the curation/review epic, while budget and scan-boundary gating before trusting a plan is the deterministic review/refresh gating epic.

claims.summary: Evaluation uses two top-level surfaces: dev for AI-assisted development work such as repo contracts, tools, and skills, and app for AI-powered product behavior such as chat, prompt, and service responses.
claims.verbatim: Evaluation uses two top-level surfaces: `dev` for AI-assisted development work such as repo contracts, tools, and skills, and `app` for AI-powered product behavior such as chat, prompt, and service responses.

claims.summary: Sample normalization inputs live in examples/starters/ and the checked-in fixture directories under fixtures/.
claims.verbatim: Sample normalization inputs live in [examples/starters/](./examples/starters/) and the checked-in fixture directories under [fixtures/](./fixtures/).

claims.summary: Cautilus treats the context-recovery case as a protected scenario kept out of tuning so the signal stays honest.
claims.verbatim: `Cautilus` treats the context-recovery case as a protected scenario kept out of tuning so the signal stays honest.
claims.edgeRationale: A protected held-out scenario is a scenarios-experiments concept, and keeping it out of tuning is the held-out-honesty boundary that constrains bounded improvement.

claims.summary: Cautilus stores the evidence in a durable file the next maintainer can reopen from disk.
claims.verbatim: It stores the evidence in a durable file the next maintainer can reopen from disk.

claims.summary: Cautilus treats packets, CLI commands, and repo instructions as agent-facing interfaces first; HTML is a human-readable mirror, not the source of truth.
claims.verbatim: - Unlike a dashboard-first review tool, `Cautilus` treats packets, CLI commands, and repo instructions as agent-facing interfaces first; HTML is a human-readable mirror, not the source of truth.

claims.summary: Cautilus does not freeze one prompt string as the contract; it treats the behavior under evaluation as the contract (intent-first).
claims.verbatim: - Unlike a prompt manager, `Cautilus` does not freeze one prompt string as the contract — it treats the behavior under evaluation as the contract (`intent-first`).

claims.summary: Cautilus separates iteration from protected validation and keeps evidence reopenable from files (held-out honesty, packet-first).
claims.verbatim: - Unlike a benchmark scrapbook, `Cautilus` separates iteration from protected validation and keeps evidence reopenable from files (`held-out honesty`, `packet-first`).
claims.edgeRationale: Separating iteration from protected validation is the held-out scenarios concept, while keeping evidence reopenable from files is the evaluate packet-first property.

claims.summary: Cautilus makes adapters, reports, review files, and compare artifacts first-class product boundaries (structured review).
claims.verbatim: - Unlike ad hoc eval scripts, `Cautilus` makes adapters, reports, review files, and compare artifacts first-class product boundaries (`structured review`).
claims.edgeRationale: Making review files first-class is the structured-review/curation epic, and making adapters, reports, and compare artifacts first-class boundaries is the evaluate surface property.

claims.summary: Cautilus keeps search and revision explicitly bounded by budgets, checkpoints, and blocked-readiness conditions (bounded autonomy).
claims.verbatim: - Unlike open-ended improver loops, `Cautilus` keeps search and revision explicitly bounded by budgets, checkpoints, and blocked-readiness conditions (`bounded autonomy`).

claims.summary: Cautilus ships a GEPA-style bounded prompt search seam above the one-shot improver, with multi-generation reflective mutation, protected reevaluation, frontier-promotion review reuse, checkpoint feedback reinjection, bounded merge synthesis, and Pareto-style frontier selection.
claims.verbatim: `Cautilus` also ships a GEPA-style bounded prompt search seam above the one-shot improver: multi-generation reflective mutation, protected reevaluation, frontier-promotion review reuse, checkpoint feedback reinjection, bounded merge synthesis, and Pareto-style frontier selection.

claims.summary: Two entry points share one host-owned cautilus-adapter.yaml and return the same durable decision surface.
claims.verbatim: Two entry points share one host-owned `cautilus-adapter.yaml` and return the same durable decision surface.
claims.edgeRationale: Returning the same durable decision surface is the evaluate output property, and sharing one host-owned adapter across the CLI and agent entry points is the install/wiring property.

claims.summary: Claude and Codex use the repo-local Cautilus Agent that cautilus init installs under .agents/skills/cautilus-agent/.
claims.verbatim: Claude and Codex use the repo-local Cautilus Agent that `cautilus init` installs under `.agents/skills/cautilus-agent/`.
claims.edgeRationale: cautilus init installing the agent at a fixed path is the install epic, and the installed surface being the agent the assistants use is the orchestration epic.

claims.summary: The minimum host-repo shape is an adapter, an installed Cautilus Agent, and run artifacts such as eval-cases.json, eval-observed.json, and eval-summary.json.
claims.verbatim: The minimum host-repo shape is an adapter, an installed Cautilus Agent, and run artifacts such as `eval-cases.json`, `eval-observed.json`, and `eval-summary.json`.

claims.summary: The result is not just a pass/fail bit: it is a set of machine-readable packets plus readable views that another maintainer or agent can reopen.
claims.verbatim: The result is not just a pass/fail bit: it is a set of machine-readable packets plus readable views that another maintainer or agent can reopen.

claims.summary: Use cautilus doctor --next-action for the next onboarding step, cautilus doctor --scope agent-surface for agent-surface discoverability, and cautilus doctor for repo wiring readiness.
claims.verbatim: Use `cautilus doctor --next-action` for the next onboarding step, `cautilus doctor --scope agent-surface` for agent-surface discoverability, and `cautilus doctor` for repo wiring readiness.

claims.summary: From this repo, npm run consumer:onboard:smoke is the shortest end-to-end adoption proof against a fresh consumer.
claims.verbatim: From this repo, `npm run consumer:onboard:smoke` is the shortest end-to-end adoption proof against a fresh consumer.
claims.edgeRationale: The smoke target being the shortest adoption proof is proven-on-itself evidence, and what it proves is fresh-consumer install/onboarding.

claims.summary: Dogfood and migration evidence lives in consumer-readiness.md, which is an evidence appendix rather than the canonical bootstrap guide.
claims.verbatim: Dogfood and migration evidence lives in [consumer-readiness.md](./docs/maintainers/consumer-readiness.md), which is an evidence appendix rather than the canonical bootstrap guide.
